zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Avis於1538年出生在英國素福爾可布拉德費爾德 聖 克萊爾時，她的父親Mr Thomas Avis29歲，母親Mrs Thomas Avis29歲。她於大約1556年與Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham在英國英格蘭素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1638年在英國素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆享年100歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Avis于1538年出生在英国素福爾可布拉德費爾德 聖 克萊爾时，她的父亲Mr Thomas Avis29岁，母亲Mrs Thomas Avis29岁。她于大约1556年与Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham在英国英格兰素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1638年在英国素福爾可厄爾 斯頓哈姆享年100岁。

nameMeanings.name: Avis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Middle English and Old French female personal name Avice, which may be from ancient Germanic Aveza, but more probably from Latin Avitia (also found in a masculine form, Avitius). In France, this surname is rare.
Portuguese and Spanish: habitational name from the little town of Avis (the stress is on the i) in Portalegre district (Portugal). This surname is found in the neighboring Spanish Extremadura.
Dutch: variant of Aafjes a metronymic from the female personal name Ave, which is of unclear origin.

variants.name: Avins
meanings: English: metronymic from the Middle English female personal name Avine, which is probably a pet form of Avis .

variants.name: Havis
meanings: English: variant of Avis with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Avison
meanings: English (Yorkshire): metronymic from the Middle English female personal name Avice (see Avis ).

variants.name: Havice
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of English   Avis  .

variants.name: Avon
meanings: habitational name from Avon (Wiltshire) or Little or Middle Avon (Gloucestershire), both named from the river on which they stand, or possibly a habitational name from a settlement on one of the rivers or small streams called Avon or Aven. These river names derive from the Celtic word for ‘river’, as reflected in Welsh afon and Gaelic abhainn. The modern surname is concentrated in Somerset and Wiltshire, England, suggesting it is associated chiefly with the Avon river that rises on the Gloucester-Wiltshire border and flows through Wiltshire and Somerset into the Severn.
from the Middle English female personal name Avine, which is probably a pet form of Avis .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Avis was born in 1538, in Bradfield St Clare, Suffolk, England, her father, Mr Thomas Avis, was 29 and her mother, Mrs Thomas Avis, was 29. She married Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham about 1556, in Earl Stonham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1638, in Earl Stonham, Suffolk, England, at the age of 100.

Elle a épousé Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham environ 1556, à Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1638, à Earl Stonham, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 100 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Avis(이)가 1538년 영국 수폴크 브래드필드 스트 클래르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Mr Thomas Avis(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs Thomas Avis(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 대략 1556년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 에를 스토함에서 Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1638년 영국 수폴크 에를 스토함에서 100세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Moses John Packard Third Earl of Stonham около 1556 г. в местности эарл стонам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1638 г. в местности эарл стонам, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 100 лет.
